question: Q4
title: Community fallback bypasses governed scope but calibrates to P1 under local single-user MCP
summary: The normal pipeline passes and filters tenant/user/agent scope, but post-pipeline community fallback calls `searchCommunities` without scope, fetches rows by ID only, and appends them. It is P0 only when scopes represent untrusted shared principals.

question: Q4
title: Causal graph tools are ID-only governed-scope gaps, not acceptable by design
summary: Public schemas expose bare memory and edge IDs with no scope fields; handlers read, insert, and delete by those IDs. Severity is P1 for local trusted-client use, P0 for shared governed stores with untrusted principals.

question: Q5
title: Fan-out summaries can mask nonzero lineage exits
summary: The worker returns nonzero subprocess exit codes as ordinary output; the pool marks any returned output fulfilled and summary success is based on fulfilled status, so failed subprocesses can produce a zero top-level exit.

question: Q5
title: Single-process fan-out can serialize and iteration overrides only affect timeout
summary: `spawnSync` inside the async worker blocks a multi-lineage fanout-run process; `iterations` is documented as a loop bound but only sizes timeout, and the prompt builder does not pass a max-iterations override.

question: Q5
title: Current review summaries are suspect even though lineage sentinels exist
summary: Eight sibling review summaries report one lineage while their ledgers record five started and five completed events. Individual stdout files include completion sentinels, so summaries are suspect certification artifacts but the current campaign is not proven to have missing lineages.
